title: Tech Slump Deepens
summary: Technology stocks closed the week sharply lower as investors reassessed the AI trade, with analysts citing rising semiconductor costs, memory pricing pressures, and questions about whether AI companies can justify soaring valuations. Bloomberg Intelligence's global head of tech research and a macro strategist discussed evolving US export restrictions on advanced AI models and a cooling IPO market. The broad repricing signals a shift from speculative enthusiasm toward selective scrutiny of AI business fundamentals.

title: How Lenovo's CFO Is Allocating Capital During One of History's Biggest Booms
summary: Lenovo's CFO describes the company's capital allocation strategy as it rides the AI infrastructure wave as a major server supplier to hyperscalers and enterprise customers building out AI training and inference capacity. The piece offers a front-row view of how major hardware vendors are thinking about the durability of the AI build-out cycle and balancing investment with margin discipline. It provides useful signal on demand durability at the hardware layer of the AI stack.

title: Apple Seeks US Approval to Buy Chips From Blacklisted CXMT
summary: Apple is pressing the White House for permission to purchase memory chips from CXMT, a blacklisted Chinese chipmaker, as it works to rein in semiconductor costs amid the AI hardware buildout. The move highlights the tension between supply chain diversification and US export control policy targeting Chinese chip manufacturers. The Financial Times first reported the executive-level talks between Apple and US government officials.

title: Building Public Life: How Bogotá and Mexico City Addressed Urban Inequality
summary: This feature documents how Bogotá and Mexico City invested in civic architecture in peripheral neighborhoods — using Mexico City's PILARES program as a central case — creating community centers that merge education, culture, and recreation where they were previously absent. The projects treat access to collective public space as a form of urban acknowledgment rather than mere infrastructure delivery. The analysis argues that cities fail not just in services but in recognition when such spaces do not exist.

title: ¿Por qué Trump 'restringió' la nueva versión de Chat GPT 5? Presiona a OpenAI para aplazar su lanzamiento
summary: OpenAI comenzó a desplegar su serie GPT-5.6 únicamente a un grupo de 20 socios aprobados por el gobierno de Trump, quien presionó para escalonar el lanzamiento por razones de seguridad nacional. La empresa reconoció que esta restricción temporal mantiene sus herramientas fuera del alcance de usuarios, desarrolladores y aliados internacionales, pero lo calificó como el camino más responsable hacia una disponibilidad más amplia en las próximas semanas. El acceso inicial opera a través de plataformas como AWS Bedrock.

title: Utility boss warns US faces blackouts due to power supply shortfall
summary: Exelon's CEO warns the US power grid faces blackout risks as AI data center demand outpaces electricity supply growth, and argues that electricity rate increases are necessary to fund the required infrastructure upgrades. The comments highlight the growing strain between AI's capital-intensive buildout and the slower pace of utility planning and regulatory approval cycles. The executive is pressing policymakers to accelerate new generation capacity approvals.

title: Las claves económicas de la semana (22 al 26 de junio): De tasas de Banxico e inflación de México y EU
summary: Banxico mantuvo su tasa de referencia en 6.5% y señaló que conservarla en ese nivel sigue siendo apropiado para el entorno macroeconómico actual. La semana incluyó publicación de datos de inflación, actividad económica de abril, empleo y balanza comercial de México, además del PCE de mayo y la revisión final del PIB del primer trimestre de EU. Adicionalmente, México colocó 6,300 millones de dólares en bonos internacionales para financiar recompra de deuda.

title: Anthropic's Mythos 5 AI Model Cleared by US for Wider Use
summary: Anthropic obtained US government approval to restore broader access to its Mythos 5 AI model after resolving Trump administration concerns about the technology's potential national security risks. The clearance ends government-imposed restrictions that had limited deployment of Anthropic's most capable system, allowing enterprise and partner access to resume. The development is a significant data point in the emerging US framework for regulating frontier AI models.

title: Forum AI CEO on Pitfalls With AI in Politics
summary: Campbell Brown, co-founder and CEO of Forum AI, argues that AI companies must subject themselves to greater external scrutiny, particularly as AI tools become embedded in political processes and public discourse. She highlights the governance gap between the pace of AI deployment and the oversight mechanisms needed to ensure accountability in politically sensitive contexts. The discussion reflects broader industry debate about voluntary versus mandatory transparency frameworks for AI in elections and media.

title: Anthropic Moves Toward Deal With US to Lift Curbs on AI Models
summary: Anthropic and the Trump administration are close to an agreement that would lift US restrictions on the company's top two AI models, following weeks of negotiations focused on national security concerns about the systems' capabilities. The deal would restore broader deployment access for models that had been subject to government-imposed limitations affecting enterprise and partner use. The development signals an emerging framework for how the US will manage advanced AI deployment alongside security review.

title: AI Trade's Bruising Week Forces Investors to Be More Selective
summary: Domain Money's founder and CEO Adam Dell discusses how the AI trade's difficult week is pushing investors toward greater selectivity, drawing on his experience at the intersection of fintech, venture capital, and early-stage startup mechanics. The conversation touches on what separates durable AI business models from those riding a speculative wave, set against broad market volatility in tech and semiconductor stocks. The discussion reflects growing investor scrutiny of the AI sector's revenue-versus-valuation math.

title: Nasdaq y S&P 500 suman cinco días de pérdidas, mientras se acelera la rotación fuera de la tecnología
summary: Las bolsas de Estados Unidos cerraron su quinta sesión consecutiva de pérdidas para el S&P 500 y el Nasdaq, marcadas por ventas en fabricantes de chips y una aceleración de la rotación hacia otros sectores del mercado. El índice S&P 500 ponderado por igual tocó un nuevo máximo histórico, señal de que el rally empieza a extenderse más allá de las mega-caps tecnológicas. Analistas describen el sentimiento como pesimista hacia las grandes tecnológicas pero optimista para el mercado en general.

title: Beyond Chips, IMF Sees AI Wealth Boom Adding to Inflation Risks
summary: The IMF's chief economist warns that AI's inflationary impact may extend beyond chip-cost pressures to include a demand-side wealth effect: as AI productivity gains make consumers wealthier and more willing to spend, aggregate demand could compound inflation risks. The analysis suggests monetary policy frameworks may need to account for AI-driven shifts in both supply capacity and consumer spending simultaneously. The IMF is studying how AI-enabled productivity interacts with central bank price stability mandates globally.

title: Apple's Vision Pro and Smart Glasses Chief to Join OpenAI
summary: Paul Meade, Apple's top executive overseeing the Vision Pro headset and smart glasses projects, is leaving for OpenAI, continuing a pattern of high-profile talent migration from established tech firms to AI-focused competitors. The defection underscores intensifying competition for hardware-AI integration expertise as companies race to develop AI-native consumer devices. The move raises questions about Apple's roadmap for spatial computing and AI-integrated wearables amid the executive transition.

title: Villa Riviera Golf Residence / Atelier130
summary: Atelier130 completed the 600-square-meter Villa Riviera Golf Residence in Abidjan, Côte d'Ivoire, integrating tropical modernism with golf-course-adjacent living through open floor plans and natural ventilation strategies suited to West Africa's climate. The project, photographed by Alejandra Loreto, demonstrates an approach to luxury residential design that responds to landscape and local climate rather than imported architectural formulas. It is the practice's 2025 signature residential project.

title: OpenAI Limits Release of New Model Under Pressure From US
summary: OpenAI is rolling out its most capable new AI model only to 20 government-approved partners before a wider release, following pressure from the Trump administration to stagger deployment over national security concerns. The company acknowledged the restriction keeps tools from developers, allies, and businesses that need them, but called the phased approach a temporary measure ahead of broader availability within weeks. Access is channeled through platforms including AWS Bedrock.

title: El dólar pone a prueba a América Latina: claves que mueven a las monedas de la región, según Citi
summary: Citi identifica los factores que determinarán el comportamiento de las monedas latinoamericanas en el segundo semestre, destacando que la región enfrenta la fortaleza del dólar con mayor resiliencia que en ciclos anteriores gracias a posturas monetarias más restrictivas y mayores reservas. Los analistas señalan que las tasas reales de mercados emergentes de alto rendimiento siguen siendo atractivas y que el contexto favorece resistencia ante el avance del dólar. El peso mexicano y otras divisas serán puestas a prueba por la política de la Fed y la evolución de materias primas.

title: AI Is Already Reshaping US Politics at Every Level
summary: Silicon Valley money and AI technology are embedded at every level of US politics ahead of the 2026 elections, from data center backlash to tech billionaire campaign funding and concerns about AI-generated deepfakes in campaign ads. The piece documents how both AI tool deployment and infrastructure backlash have become central electoral issues across federal, state, and local races. Regulatory proposals targeting algorithmic manipulation and AI-generated political content are advancing in multiple states.

title: Exclusiva: No nos obsesiona salir a bolsa sino ser un motor del emprendimiento — gerente de Rappi en Colombia
summary: El country manager de Rappi en Colombia descarta la urgencia de una IPO y afirma que el verdadero objetivo es consolidarse como motor del emprendimiento en Latinoamérica, priorizando el crecimiento en ciudades intermedias y estratos medios. La startup, con presencia en nueve países, ya genera EBITDA positivo y crece más del 30% anual, reinvirtiendo el 100% de sus utilidades. La estrategia de expansión hacia mercados menos saturados posiciona a Rappi para una eventual salida a bolsa sin la presión inmediata de ejecutarla.

title: How a New Generation of Architects Is Designing with Natural Light
summary: This piece traces how contemporary architects are inheriting and evolving the tradition of treating natural light as a primary design material, citing works from Tadao Ando and Alvar Aalto as lineage references for current practitioners. Through the VELUX program presentations, it shows how daylight shapes spatial experience, comfort, and the perception of time within buildings. The article frames natural light not as a sustainability metric but as an irreplaceable architectural substance that defines presence.

title: Snøhetta Reimagines Aino and Alvar Aalto's Paimio Sanatorium as a Wellness and Cultural Destination
summary: Snøhetta has developed a masterplan to transform the UNESCO-candidate Paimio Sanatorium (1929–1933) in Finland into a destination combining hospitality, wellness programming, cultural spaces, and international dialogue venues. The adaptive reuse proposal honors Alvar Aalto's modernist patient-centered design principles while introducing new programming layers suited to contemporary use. A UNESCO decision on the 'Aalto Works' nomination is expected in July 2026.

title: WIDOARQUITECTOS Office / WIDOARQUITECTOS
summary: WIDOARQUITECTOS completed their own 150-square-meter studio in Cuernavaca, Mexico, as a self-designed project delivered in 2026. The office blends functional workspace requirements with the aesthetic sensibilities of contemporary Mexican architecture, photographed by César Belio. The project reflects a growing trend of Mexican practices designing their own environments as a live demonstration of their design philosophy.
